data.title: Treasury issues GENIUS Act rule proposal, seeks comment on state oversight for smaller issuers
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury Department is once again seeking public comment on the implementation of the GENIUS Act, the legislation passed into law last summer that created guidelines for stablecoin issuance and usage in the United States. 

On Wednesday, the Treasury issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (NPRM) representing the "first regulation Treasury has proposed to implement the GENIUS Act," according to a statement on Wednesday. 

"The GENIUS Act directs Treasury to, through notice and comment rulemaking, establish broad-based principles for determining whether a state-level regulatory regime is substantially similar to the federal regulatory framework under the GENIUS Act," the agency wrote.

Wednesday’s NPRM proposes broad-based principles for determining whether a state-level regulatory regime is "substantially similar" to the federal regulatory framework established by the GENIUS Act.

The proposal is meant to address a GENIUS Act provision that would allow smaller stablecoin issuers (those with less than $10 billion in issued assets) to opt for state regulation instead of full federal oversight — but only if the state regime meets the "substantially similar" standard. 

Members of the public will have 60 days to respond to the Treasury’s NPRM in the Federal Register. 

Federal regulators are still working to interpret the high-level guidelines established by the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins (GENIUS) Act, including how it interacts with existing money transmission regulations and where particular agencies will maintain oversight.

Seeking public remarks

This is not the first time the Treasury has sought public comment related to GENIUS.

The department issued a request for comment related to digital forensic tools and stablecoins in August, shortly after the law was passed, and a separate Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking seeking broad input on implementation, including tax-related and information gathering issues, in September. 

Top U.S. banking regulators, the FDIC and OCC, have also published notices of proposed rulemaking. 

Notably, the GENIUS Act did not include guidelines for yield-bearing stablecoins, a topic that has become a serious roadblock as Congress attempts to pass broader market structure legislation, often referred to as Clarity, after one particular House draft.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

data.title: Micron's stock is seeing its biggest gain in a year, en route to a record market-cap boost
data.description:
By Emily Bary

Micron has added $43.8 billion to its market value so far on Wednesday

Evercore's C.J. Muse is bullish on Micron's stock, but acknowledged that it's become the subject of fierce debate.

Micron Technology's stock is staging a strong rebound after slipping into bear-market territory last week.

Shares of the memory-chip company (MU) were surging 10.9% in Wednesday trading, after rising 5% in Tuesday action. The Wednesday gain could be Micron's largest since an 18.8% rally on April 9, 2025, if it holds through the close. The stock was leading S&P 500 SPX gainers for the day.

Micron had added $43.8 billion in market capitalization based on intraday action on Wednesday, putting it on course for its biggest one-day haul on record, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

As investors see the war in Iran potentially edging nearer to completion, that's benefited so-called risk-on trades, including within the memory sector. Sandisk's stock (SNDK) was up 10.5% on the day, while Western Digital shares (WDC) were up 10.6% and Seagate Technology's stock (STX) was up 7.7%.

Those moves come after South Korean memory stocks Samsung Electronics (KR:005930) and SK Hynix (KR:000660) rose more than 13% and 10% in Asian trading.

Micron and its rivals have benefited greatly from a memory supply crunch that has sent product prices soaring. But Evercore ISI analyst C.J. Muse noted before Wednesday's open that Micron's stock was trading at less than 3.5 times its expected earnings-per-share potential north of $100 by calendar-year 2027.

His recent meetings with Micron's management team focused on "the secular and durable nature of the business, how high-value-add memory road maps only augment AI capabilities and how this backdrop is driving structural changes to Micron's customer supply relationships," Muse wrote.

Investors, though, seem "dubious about these changes," hence the stock's low valuation on a price-to-earnings basis.

Read: Micron's stock is spectacularly cheap, as these numbers show

While Muse has an overweight rating and top-pick designation on Micron's stock, he acknowledged there could be an intellectual "tug of war" around the stock, as investors weigh improving fundamentals against what some view as a looming sense that the current boom cycle will come crashing down.

The memory market is historically cyclical, and some on Wall Street seem spooked by awareness of past cycles in which oversupply eroded industry profits. But is artificial intelligence a new twist that could structurally improve market dynamics over a sustained period?

"This is an obvious area of debate for investors and one where both sides have very pointed views," Muse wrote. "From Micron's perspective, they definitely see signs that suggest this time is indeed different. AI and the data center are driving the current inflection, making memory more of a strategic asset."

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: GE, Boeing, and 3 More Aerospace and Defense Stocks to Buy — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Aerospace and defense stocks have both been beaten up by the Iran war, especially higher oil prices. Now, commercial aerospace is a better bet than defensestocks, according to Wells Fargo.

Analyst David Strauss launched coverage of the sector on Wednesday.

"We see further aero outperformance on strong relative earnings per share growth that more than offsets potential valuation downside," he wrote.

Strauss figures earnings can double by the end of the decade, driven by chronic undersupply of new planes, despite rising oil prices.

Boeing and Airbus delivered an average of 1,500 planes a year in 2016, 2017, and 2018. That collapsed to fewer than 1,000 in 2020 and 2021. The pair delivered almost 1,500 jets in 2025, and deliveries are expected to grow to 1,900 by 2028.

"Defense has upside to growth expectations, while valuation is reasonable in [the] context of prior periods of high budgets and heightened geopolitics," he said.

After the midterm elections, however,

budget outlooks will get "more difficult" and large contractors will face " heightened competition along with increased CapEx demands that could negatively affect free cash flow and shareholder returns."

Military spending around the globe is increasing, but traditional prime contractors are facing competition from so-called neo-primes that want to disrupt the sector with low-cost, autonomous battle systems.

Four commercial aerospace stocks he rates Buy are engine makers GE Aerospace, Rolls-Royce, parts supplier Woodward, and Boeing.

"GE has significantly exceeded its margin forecast over the last several years, and we see further upside," he wrote.

Strauss sees GE's LEAP engine, which powers the A320 family of aircraft and the Boeing 737 MAX, becoming more profitable than the older CFM56 engines.

His price target for GE is $325. The stock was up 3.8% at $294.42 in midday trading; the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were up 1.1% and 0.9%, respectively.

For Boeing, Strauss has a $250 price target. Shares were at $209, up 5%, in midday trading. His Woodward product is $440. Shares were at $377.04, up 5.3%. His Rolls-Royce target is GBP13.50. Shares were at GBP12.07 in overseas trading, up 6.6%.

Northrop Grumman is Strauss' favorite defense contractor. The company "has invested ahead of need relative to peers, and we think [it] outgrows," he wrote.

The U.S. government is looking to dramatically increase the production of missiles and drones. Northrop makes a lot of those, including the Global Hawk drone and SiAWs, or Stand-in Attack Weapons.

His Northrop target price is $800. Shares were at $701.09, up 2.8%, in midday trading.

Strauss' aerospace picks are popular with his peers. Overall, 84% of analysts covering GE Aerospace, Woodward, Rolls, and Boeing rate shares Buy, according to FactSet. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 typically ranges from 55% to 60%.

Northrop's support looks reasonable, with 54% of analysts rating its stock Buy.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Commencement Bank Welcomes New Commercial Healthcare Banking Team
data.description:
TACOMA, WA / ACCESS Newswire / April 1, 2026 / Commencement Bank, a subsidiary of Commencement Bancorp, Inc. (OTCQX:CBWA), is pleased to announce that Dani Collins, Tom Reuter, and Val Williams have joined Commencement following longstanding positions at Columbia Bank. The new team will be located at the Bank's headquarters in Tacoma, Wash. with Collins as Senior Vice President and Healthcare Banking Team Leader. Reuter will serve alongside Collins as Vice President and Healthcare Banking Officer, while Williams will oversee the team's daily operations as their Loan Administrator. With over 35 years of combined experience, they are widely known as the premier healthcare banking group in the Pacific Northwest. This team brings a wealth of commercial and healthcare banking knowledge and expertise along with a solid and expansive reputation for cultivating strong, long-lasting relationships.

Collins joins Commencement with more than 25 years of banking experience primarily focused on healthcare banking and commercial real estate lending. In her most recent role, she served as Senior Vice President and Healthcare Market Director of Washington and Northern Idaho. She holds a degree from the University of Washington with a focus on Finance, Economics, and Organizational Development. Dani brings a strong commitment to community involvement, focused on volunteerism and board experience across healthcare non-profits dedicated to advocacy and cancer research.

"I am deeply committed to being a trusted resource and advisor to healthcare providers so they can focus on delivering quality care to the communities they serve. I believe that when providers have the right financial partners supporting them, our communities prosper. I chose to join Commencement Bank because of their genuine commitment to strengthening the local economy and the communities they serve. As a community bank, they understand that investing in local businesses and families creates lasting impact. This shared sense of purpose speaks directly to the heart of our team's commitment to partnering with healthcare professionals to help them build healthier, stronger communities," said Collins, SVP, Healthcare Banking Team Leader.

With eight years of experience in healthcare banking and a degree from Gonzaga University in Business Administration, Reuter's strength in production and portfolio management, and his commitment to serving the healthcare community, will be a significant contribution to the Bank. Reuter's dedication to remaining current with the healthcare industry and evolving regulatory environment has been instrumental in growing his client base, earning their trust through sound financial guidance and responsiveness.

Williams comes to us with over 14 years in banking, with the most recent eight years supporting Collins and Reuter operationally with experience in credit analysis, loan documentation, and overall client management. Her reputation for excellent customer service makes her a solid addition to the overall Commencement team.

"As a local community bank, we are deeply committed to our clients, and we take great pride in being a trusted financial partner. That's why I'm thrilled to welcome Dani, Tom, and Val to the Commencement Bank team. Their dedication to our community and their clients reflects our core mission, and their expertise will immediately strengthen our organization. This well-known and respected team will amplify our impact in the markets we serve," said John E. Manolides, Chief Executive Officer.

"We are thrilled to welcome this exceptional banking team to our organization. Their deep industry expertise and commitment to relationship driven service align perfectly with our values. The addition of this team represents a strategic investment in both expertise and service excellence, and we look forward to the meaningful contributions they will bring to our organization, said Nigel L. English, President & Chief Operating Officer.

Collins, Reuter, and Williams began their roles in late March 2026 at the Bank's Tacoma headquarters. They will remain focused on serving the Washington and Northern Idaho markets and will specialize in full-service banking for medical, veterinary, and dental practices.

###

Dani Collins

Senior Vice President and Healthcare Banking Team Leader

Phone: 253.831.9023 or dcollins@commencementbank.com

Tom Reuter

Vice President and Healthcare Banking Officer

Phone: 253.363.3709 or treuter@commencementbank.com

Val Williams

Loan Administrator II

Phone: 253.284.1843 or vwilliams@commencementbank.com

About Commencement Bank

Commencement Bank, subsidiary of Commencement Bancorp, Inc. (OTCQX: CBWA), is headquartered in Tacoma, Washington, and was formed in 2006 to provide traditional, reliable, and sustainable banking in Pierce, South King, Kitsap, and Thurston counties and the surrounding areas. Their team of banking experts focuses on personal attention, flexible service, and building strong relationships with customers through state-of-the-art technology as well as traditional delivery systems. As a local bank, Commencement Bank is deeply committed to the community. For more information, please visit www.commencementbank.com. For information related to the trading of CBWA, please visit www.otcmarkets.com.

Contact Information:

John E. Manolides, Chief Executive Officer | 253.284.1802

Nigel English, President and Chief Operating Officer | 253.284.1801

SOURCE: Commencement Bank

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Conagra Returns to Organic Sales Growth, But Inflation Pressures Profit — Update
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Conagra Brands narrowed its annual forecast amid mixed quarterly results, as strength in frozen meals and snacks drove organic sales growth and inflation weighed on profit.

The maker of Healthy Choice frozen meals and Snack Pack pudding cups said on Wednesday it now expects organic sales--which account for revenue generated from existing operations--to be roughly flat for the year. That compares with a prior outlook ranging from down 1% to up 1%.

Conagra has been working to return to organic sales growth, after recent quarters were pressured by supply constraints. Organic sales rose 2.4% during the latest quarter, driven by both higher volumes and prices, the food-and-snacks company said.

Supply has improved and consumers are currently willing to pay higher prices for canned goods and sweets such as Swiss Miss hot chocolate. The company raised prices across its portfolio last quarter, and so far, the increases haven't hurt demand, Chief Executive Sean Connolly said on a call with analysts.

Still, inflation remained elevated at about 7% during the recent quarter, inclusive of both core inflation and gross tariff expense, Conagra said. Brands such as Swiss Miss have seen high commodity costs for cocoa, while canned products have been affected by the imposition of levies on steel and aluminum.

Inflationary pressures, coupled with weaker contributions from its Ardent Mills joint venture, prompted Conagra to forecast adjusted earnings for the year of approximately $1.70 a share, the low end of its prior range of $1.70 to $1.85 a share.

For its quarter ended Feb. 22, Conagra posted a profit of $199.8 million, or 42 cents a share, compared with $145.1 million, or 30 cents a share, a year earlier. Stripping out one-time items, earnings were 39 cents a share, just shy of analysts' expectations of 40 cents, according to FactSet.

Sales slipped 1.9%, to $2.79 billion, but came in ahead of Wall Street estimates for $2.76 billion.

Growth was driven by strong performance in frozen foods and snacks, where the company said it is gaining market share and surpassing category growth. Snacks outpaced their broader category, while frozen volumes rebounded following last year's supply disruptions.

Sales across Conagra's grocery and snacks unit fell 6.3%. The decline was partially offset by a 1.6% increase in sales from its refrigerated and frozen business.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Inside the "high-revision regime" distorting the US labor market
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes advance; Nasdaq out front, up ~1.8%
  • Comm Svcs lead S&P sector gainers; Energy tumbles on falling crude
  • Dollar falls ~0.6%; US crude down >1%; bitcoin gains >1%; gold up >2%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield edges down to ~4.30%

INSIDE THE "HIGH-REVISION REGIME" DISTORTING THE US LABOR MARKET

U.S. labor market data is central to Federal Reserve policy and a cornerstone of macro analysis. But a troubling pattern has emerged over the past few years: payroll numbers keep getting revised lower, and by a lot.

Ryan Kimmel, a macro asset allocation strategist at DoubleLine Capital, says the labor market has entered what he calls "a high-revision regime" — one where initial releases carry less informational value than in the past.

Cumulative downward revisions since early 2023 now total approximately 2.77 million jobs, representing the gap between what was initially reported and what subsequent revisions revealed.

The pattern isn't random. Negative revisions in the two months following initial releases have dominated since 2022, reflecting what Kimmel describes as "a persistent, unidirectional skew rather than episodic and random changes." The practical effect is that real-time estimates have consistently overstated underlying momentum.

The household survey tells a similar story. Total employment now runs roughly 1.6 million below earlier estimates, which Kimmel calls "a downward reassessment of the employment level and participation dynamics," not merely monthly noise.

In light of this, "Confidence bands around first-time prints probably should be wider than previously assumed," Kimmel concludes. And in a high-revision regime, conviction comes not from any single report but from the consistency of trends across multiple datasets over time.

data.title: Nike's Guidance Tests Wall Street's Patience — Market Talk
data.description: Nike's projection for further pain ahead is prompting BofA Securities analysts to sit out the next few quarters as they expect a longer path to recovery. The analysts had previously forecast Nike would return to growth in the fiscal first quarter given improved innovation in performance products and lapping turnaround actions from the prior year, but the company projected sales declines into the fiscal third quarter. "Strong results in running and NA were the reasons for our patience but with the sales inflection now nine months away, we see little room for multiple expansion," the analysts say, downgrading the stock to neutral from buy. They also cut their price target to $55 from $73. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Exxon Mobil on Pace For Largest One-Day Market Cap Loss Since 2008 — WSJ
data.description:
By Jared Mitovich

One of early 2026's biggest successes in financial markets is off to a rough start in the second quarter.

Exxon Mobil has lost around $36 billion in market cap so far on Wednesday-on pace for its largest one-day market cap loss since the global financial crisis in 2008, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The S&P 500 energy sector was also down amid investor hopes for an end to the war in Iran that would bring relief to oil prices.

Shares of Exxon Mobil were down around 5% in early afternoon trading, nearing their worst one-day percentage decline in almost a year. Energy stocks had a strong performance in the first quarter, which was bookended by one jolt to the oil market-the capture of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro-and another, far larger one: a weekslong conflict with Iran that's effectively shuttered the Strait of Hormuz, a key global maritime chokepoint for energy.

Exxon Mobil gets roughly one-fifth of its oil-and-gas production from the Middle East, analysts estimate, and is poised to lose about $5 billion in revenue a year after Iran's missile strikes damaged Qatar's natural-gas facilities, The Wall Street Journal previously reported.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: RAY—ALLEN Expands Nexa Platform Suite With Acquisition of Paratira Ltd
data.description:
The integration of Paratira's AI-driven platform to NexaONE(TM) delivers immediate value for MSP partners, including faster onboarding and next-level insights for (Day 2) Managed Services operations

CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 01, 2026--

RAY--ALLEN, Inc. ("RAY--ALLEN"), a Chicago-based enterprise technology firm with over 21 years of service to MSPs/SPs, VARs, and Systems Integrators across the global IT channel, as well as Fortune 1000 organizations, today announced the completion of two simultaneous transactions: the acquisition of the enterprise platform technology and customer assets of Paratira Limited, and a minority strategic investment from Datatec plc ( / ), a $3.6B global ICT Operator with 40 years of operating history across more than 50 countries.

Datatec plc has made a minority equity investment in RAY--ALLEN, establishing a formal strategic partnership. Datatec is a global ICT Operator, not a financial investor. Their portfolio includes Logicalis International and Westcon-Comstor, which together represent significant global enterprise channel depth and a longstanding Cisco ecosystem partnership. RAY--ALLEN remains fully independently operated under its existing leadership.

The transaction expands the Nexa Platform Suite across two new capability paths. Path 1 delivers a Sales Enablement entry tier with a streamlined, lower lift onboarding experience and near-instant partner value. Path 2 introduces a Day 2 intelligence capability that converts MSP operational and observability data into real-time technical support insights, reducing resolution time and surfacing issues before they become incidents, launching with a Paratira-built LogicMonitor connector and a roadmap of additional platform integrations. Together, they make Nexa easier to start and more powerful to run on.

Transaction Highlights

  • RAY--ALLEN acquires the intellectual property, platform technology, and customer assets of Paratira Limited, a UK-registered enterprise technology company whose platform was built and proven in large-scale enterprise MSP production environments, giving RAY--ALLEN immediate first-party ownership of both capability paths and the Paratira-built LogicMonitor connector.
  • The Nexa Platform Suite gains a Sales Enablement entry tier with streamlined onboarding and a Day 2 intelligence layer that enables MSP partners to activate their operational data, delivering faster resolutions and fewer incidents across managed service environments.
  • Datatec plc makes a minority equity investment in RAY--ALLEN, creating global ICT Operator-level support as RAY--ALLEN scales the Nexa Platform Suite. Datatec brings $3.6B in revenue, 40 years of enterprise operating history, and deep relationships within the Cisco ecosystem.
  • RAY--ALLEN remains independently operated under its existing leadership team. The minority strategic investment does not alter RAY--ALLEN's operating structure, partner relationships, or strategic direction. Visit rayalleninc.com for more information.

Leadership Commentary

"RAY--ALLEN has been the partner the global IT channel trusts to drive revenue growth. With the addition of the Paratira platform, we are redefining that role--moving beyond growth into how partners operate and make decisions. The new entry tier delivers immediate Sales insight with minimal friction, and our Day 2 intelligence capabilities signal a clear shift: RAY--ALLEN is now operating inside the intelligence layer of MSP infrastructure. We are ready to engage with partners across the global IT channel on this next phase of the platform and look forward to the conversation."

Todd Ringleman, Chief Executive Officer, RAY--ALLEN, Inc.

"I'm delighted to welcome RAY--ALLEN, Inc. into the Datatec Group portfolio. By combining Paratira's capabilities with the Nexa suite, we are expanding a market-leading platform with the core capabilities and insights needed to elevate MSPs in the AI era. This integrated offering positions RAY--ALLEN well for sustained growth and long-term value creation for customers and shareholders."

Jens Montanana, Chief Executive Officer, Datatec

"We built Paratira to turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insights, so MSPs can move faster, prove value, and drive better outcomes for customers. With RAY--ALLEN, Inc. acquiring Paratira and integrating our AI-driven platform into NexaONE(TM), we're delivering immediate value for MSP partners: faster onboarding and next-level operational insight for day-to-day managed services. Equally, it sets our platform and team on the path to something bigger: to be part of an established ecosystem and platform suite customers can start small with and expand out as they scale."

Toby Alcock, Chief Executive Officer, Paratira Limited

About RAY--ALLEN, Inc.

RAY--ALLEN, Inc. is a Chicago-based enterprise technology firm with over 21 years of service to MSPs/SPs, VARs, and Systems Integrators across the global IT channel, as well as Fortune 1000 organizations. The Nexa Platform Suite delivers Sales Enablement, Customer Success, and AI-driven Customer Experience capabilities designed to help partners grow revenue, retain customers, and, through the Paratira acquisition, activate operational data for Day 2 technical intelligence. For more information, visit rayalleninc.com.

About Datatec plc

Datatec ( / ) is a $3.6B global ICT Operator established in 1986 and headquartered in South Africa. Operating across more than 50 countries on six continents with over 11,000 employees, Datatec's divisions include Westcon-Comstor, a global technology distribution business and Cisco ecosystem partner, and Logicalis International, a UK-based global IT solutions and managed services provider. For more information, visit datatec.com.

About Paratira Limited

Paratira Limited is a UK-registered enterprise technology company whose platform was developed with a focus on solving real-world MSP operational challenges at scale. The platform delivers two capability paths now integrated into the RAY--ALLEN Nexa Platform Suite: a Sales Enablement entry tier designed for streamlined deployment and near-instant partner value, and a Day 2 intelligence layer that activates MSP observability and infrastructure data for technical support use cases. For more information, visit paratira.ai.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains statements that are forward-looking in nature. These statements involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Neither RAY--ALLEN, Inc. nor Datatec plc undertakes any obligation to update forward-looking statements to reflect changed assumptions, the occurrence of unanticipated events, or changes in future operating results over time.

RAY ALLEN, Inc.

data.title: deBridge - MCP Update - 01 April 2026
data.description:
As described in the deBridge team’s MCP upgrade update, the new MCP server, composable skills, and agent discovery layer allow any browser-based agent to use deBridge for cross-chain actions such as trading and bridging. This shifts deBridge toward being automated infrastructure for AI and programmatic agents rather than only human users. If agents integrate deBridge as a default routing layer, protocol transaction volumes and fee flows could scale with agent usage. To the extent that network activity and governance are tied to DBR, higher protocol utilization can support token demand, but this depends on real developer adoption.

data.title: Venice Token - Bithumb Listing - 01 April 2026
data.description:
According to the Bithumb listing announcement, Venice Token (VVV) will list on Bithumb with a VVV/KRW spot pair at 3 PM local time, giving Korean traders direct fiat access. The mechanism is straightforward: adding a KRW pair on a major domestic exchange can boost liquidity, tighten spreads, and enable new capital inflows from retail users who could not easily access VVV before. Listing events often create short-term order-book imbalances as early holders gain exit liquidity while new buyers speculate on the narrative, leading to elevated volatility around and shortly after the go-live time.

data.title: Inflation Expectations Rise in Bank of Mexico Survey — Market Talk
data.description: Analysts surveyed in March by the Bank of Mexico raised their inflation expectations for this year, while keeping the economic growth forecast unchanged at 1.5%. Inflation is seen ending 2026 at 4.21%, according to the median estimate of 41 analysts, up from 4% in the February survey. The year-end estimate for core inflation edged up to 4.2% from 4.17%. The Bank of Mexico raised its own inflation forecasts last week for the first three quarters of this year. The central bank cut its benchmark interest rate to 6.75% from 7%, and said it still expects inflation to reach its 3% target in 2Q 2027. Analysts in the survey kept their year-end estimate for the benchmark interest rate at 6.5%.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: US natural gas stockpiles likely rose by 34 billion cubic feet last week
data.description:
U.S. energy firms likely added 34 billion cubic feet of natural gas into storage last week, according to the average estimate in a Reuters poll released on Wednesday.

The estimate compares with an injection of 30 bcf during the same week a year ago and an average decline of four bcf over the past five years (2021-2025) for the week.

In the prior week ended March 20, utilities withdrew 54 bcf of gas from storage. (USOILN=ECI)

If correct, the forecast for the week ended March 27 would increase stockpiles to 1.863 trillion cubic feet. That would be about 5.3% above the same week a year ago and about 2.9% over the five-year average for the week.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release its weekly storage report at 10:30 a.m. EDT (1430 GMT) on Thursday.

There were about 76 heating degree days last week, compared with a 30-year normal of 112 for the period, data from financial firm LSEG showed.

HDDs measure the number of degrees a day's average temperature is below 65 degrees Fahrenheit (18 degrees Celsius) to estimate demand to heat homes and businesses.

Reuters polled 12 analysts, whose estimates ranged from injections of seven bcf to 42 bcf, with a median increase of 35 bcf.

Early estimates for the week ending April 3 ranged from injections of 32 bcf to 59 bcf, with an average increase of 41 bcf.

Those figures compare with an injection of 53 bcf during the same week last year and a five-year average increase of 13 bcf.

data.title: FTC Release: FTC Secures Settlement Against Xponential Fitness for Franchise Rule Violations
data.description:
Fitness franchisor to pay $17 million for allegedly misrepresenting key information in franchise disclosures including time to open and operate studios

The Federal Trade Commission has secured a settlement against Xponential Fitness for Franchise Rule violations and related deceptive practices, including $17 million that will be returned to franchisees, which is the largest amount ever to go back to consumers in a franchise case.

View Press Release: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2026/03/ftc-secures-settlement-against-xponential-fitness-franchise-rule-violations

data.title: Indian Bank Q4 update: Total business rises 13% YoY, deposits cross ₹8.3 lakh cr
data.description:
Indian Bank reported a steady business update for the March quarter, with growth across deposits and advances, while the CASA ratio remained broadly stable.

The bank’s CASA ratio stood at 39.86%, compared to 39.08% in the December quarter and 40.17% a year ago.

Total business came in at ₹14.98 lakh crore, rising 13.1% year-on-year and 4.8% sequentially.

Advances grew 13.6% YoY and 4.5% QoQ to ₹6.68 lakh crore during the quarter.

Total deposits increased 12.6% on-year and 4.9% sequentially to ₹8.30 lakh crore.

On the asset side, RAM (Retail, Agriculture, MSME) advances stood at ₹4.06 lakh crore, up 15.7% YoY and 4.1% QoQ.

Indian Bank is a major public sector bank headquartered in Chennai, offering comprehensive retail, corporate, MSME, agricultural, and international banking services.

Also read: Indian Bank Q3 Results: Stock gains over 5% after slippages improve, double-digit business growth

Shares of Indian Bank ended today’s trading session 4.7% higher at ₹886 on NSE, in line with the rally in the broader market. On a year-to-date basis, the stock is up 6.4%.

data.title: Philip Morris Stock Drops. Nicotine Pouches' FDA Review Is Delayed, Report Says. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Philip Morris International was among the biggest decliners in the S&P 500 index on Wednesday, after a report said the Food and Drug Administration's fast-track review to clear certain nicotine pouches for sale in the U.S. has been delayed.

The FDA has yet to complete a fast-track review process for certain nicotine pouches, including some Philip Morris products, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing three sources. Scientists at the agency are hesitant to authorize the products due to potential risks to children and new users, the report said.

The FDA's fast-track review was originally expected to be finalized in December 2025. What this review process means is that products that were on the market pre-2022 can continue to be sold pending the FDA review, but newer products cannot be sold until they receive approval.

"We believe in the strength of the scientific substantiation supporting our applications, which have been at the agency for some time," a Philip Morris International spokesperson told Barron's via email.

The FDA did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Barron's.

Shares of Philip Morris, the cigarette and Zyn nicotine pouch maker, sank 5.9% $155.65 on Wednesday, continuing a downward trajectory after finishing March down 11.5%.

For comparison, the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average on Wednesday rose 0.9% and 0.8%, respectively.

Jefferies analysts Andrei Andon-Ionita and Edward Mundy wrote Wednesday that Philip Morris International's Zyn is the largest nicotine pouch brand in the U.S., with more than 55% volume share in 2025.

To their point, Philip Morris reported that fourth-quarter Zyn shipments rose by 19% to 196 million cans. The tobacco pouches have become a key product for the company in recent years, helping offset a drop in cigarette sales volumes.

"Given expectations imply a Zyn recovery, fueled by the introduction of new products, we would see a delay as a negative for PM," the Jefferies analysts wrote of the reported delay of the fast-track review.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
